notes: Primitives define stable capability surfaces and deterministic bindings.
Primary runtime ontology is core/exec/data/graph/knowledge; legacy boot/ingress labels are compatibility aliases only.

primitives.name: Lifecycle
primitives.definition: Stack lifecycle operations exposed to operators.

primitives.name: Inspect
primitives.definition: Read-only diagnostics and streams.

primitives.name: Verify
primitives.definition: Verification and test gates.

primitives.name: Control Plane
primitives.definition: Control-plane entrypoints aligned to core/exec/data/graph/knowledge; legacy ingress/boot labels are compatibility aliases.

primitives.name: Pack
primitives.definition: Installable/versioned domain pack unit (policy+trials+contracts).

primitives.name: Trial
primitives.definition: Executable qualification unit: input → Run + records + metrics.

primitives.name: Plan
primitives.definition: Declarative wave plan executable deterministically.

primitives.name: Runner
primitives.definition: Executes Trial/Plan, emits canonical records, applies validate fail-fast.

primitives.name: Emitter
primitives.definition: Materializes records/artifacts and links refs/hashes deterministically.

primitives.name: Bundle Assemble
primitives.definition: Assemble bundle payloads and manifests for governed delivery.

primitives.name: Verification Run
primitives.definition: Execute deterministic verification workflow and emit evidence pointers.

primitives.name: Job
primitives.definition: Scheduled execution with stable status and logs.

primitives.name: Report
primitives.definition: Human+machine output derived from verified artifacts (index, findings).

primitives.name: Boot Plane
primitives.definition: Boot bring-up and boot-time verification surface.

primitives.name: Ingress Plane
primitives.definition: Control-plane entrypoint for handshake, session coordination and routing.

primitives.name: Core Plane
primitives.definition: Core authority surface: workspace lifecycle and core-bound operations.

primitives.name: Exec Plane
primitives.definition: Exec execution surface: exec status and execution-adjacent control.

primitives.name: Knowledge Plane
primitives.definition: Knowledge support surface: memory/cognition/provider-adjacent control endpoints.

primitives.name: Data Plane
primitives.definition: Data-plane governance surface for records/stores/query and lifecycle controls.

primitives.name: Graph Plane
primitives.definition: Graph-plane governance surface for relational truth/materialization/lineage summaries.

primitives.name: Namespace
primitives.definition: Canonical namespace for types/resources; stabilizes ABI and prevents collisions.

primitives.name: Ref
primitives.definition: Canonical reference to a resource/artifact (URI-like) deterministically resolvable.

primitives.name: Key
primitives.definition: Identity/key for signing or attestation (v1 may be fingerprint-only).

primitives.name: Hash
primitives.definition: Content identity (content-address) for immutability, sealing, deduplication.

primitives.name: Artifact
primitives.definition: Materialized object with role/ref/hash; minimum unit of proof.

primitives.name: Store
primitives.definition: Local store for artifacts indexed by ref/hash.

primitives.name: Schema
primitives.definition: Versioned shape contract (JSON Schema) for normative artifacts.

primitives.name: Validate
primitives.definition: Deterministic validation: schema + cross-file consistency; fail-fast.

primitives.name: Clock
primitives.definition: Timestamp rules and normalization for stable ordering.

primitives.name: Record
primitives.definition: Append-only record stream (json/jsonl) with canonical header.

primitives.name: Timeline
primitives.definition: Ordered record sequence with continuity invariants.

primitives.name: Metric
primitives.definition: Typed measurement (value+unit+window) aggregable and comparable.

primitives.name: Index
primitives.definition: Machine-readable inventory of artifacts (role→path/hash/ref).

primitives.name: Manifest
primitives.definition: Ingress document describing a collection and its hashes (bundle/wave/run).

primitives.name: Selector
primitives.definition: Deterministic rules to select runs/evidence for publication sets.

primitives.name: Channel
primitives.definition: Controlled emission channels (stdout/stderr/log/eventbus) with stable formats.

primitives.name: Fingerprint
primitives.definition: Environment fingerprint (OS/toolchain/deps) for audit and comparability.

primitives.name: Versioning
primitives.definition: Compatibility rules and migrations; prevents silent breakage.

primitives.name: Path
primitives.definition: Canonical path normalization within a declared ingress (anti-traversal).

primitives.name: File
primitives.definition: Controlled file IO with stable stat semantics.

primitives.name: Dir
primitives.definition: Directory operations with deterministic ordering for walk/treehash.

primitives.name: Stream
primitives.definition: Byte/record stream with stable framing (pipe-like).

primitives.name: Lock
primitives.definition: Deterministic mutual exclusion for build/verify critical resources.

primitives.name: Txn
primitives.definition: Atomic update unit for manifest/index/store changes.

primitives.name: Error
primitives.definition: Standard error type (code/class/message/context) for UX and CI.

primitives.name: Status
primitives.definition: Operational status model (pending/running/done/failed) persistable.

primitives.name: Resource
primitives.definition: Resource descriptor (CPU/RAM/disk/net) with units and limits.

primitives.name: Quota
primitives.definition: Quantitative constraint (size/time/count) enforceable and verifiable.

primitives.name: Sandbox
primitives.definition: Execution boundary (fs/net/proc) inspectable and enforceable.

primitives.name: Capability
primitives.definition: Runtime feature/permission (can-do) distinct from authority/policy (should-do).

primitives.name: Endpoint
primitives.definition: Canonical endpoint (proto/host/port) for matching policy and logging.

primitives.name: Route
primitives.definition: Routing/logging rule surface for traffic (debug/audit).

primitives.name: Codec
primitives.definition: Declared/versioned codec (json/jsonl/cbor…) with stable identifiers.

primitives.name: Seed
primitives.definition: Deterministic seed for run/replay; makes choices reproducible.

primitives.name: Provenance
primitives.definition: Lineage graph for artifacts (inputs→outputs) traceable and queryable.

primitives.name: Journal
primitives.definition: Append-only journal for critical operations (txn/sealing) for audit.

primitives.name: Checkpoint
primitives.definition: Restorable checkpoint for long executions (runner).

primitives.name: Diff
primitives.definition: Deterministic diff between two artifacts/trees/manifests.

primitives.name: Patch
primitives.definition: Controlled patch application with audit trail.

primitives.name: Event
primitives.definition: Recorded occurrence entering governance evaluation; append-only.

primitives.name: Identity
primitives.definition: Stable identity for actor/component/workload attribution.

primitives.name: Authority
primitives.definition: Explicit right (scope+constraints) to attempt an Effect.

primitives.name: Contract
primitives.definition: Versioned rule (constraints + required evidence) referenceable by id.

primitives.name: Baseline
primitives.definition: Immutable operational profile derived from Contract (default behavior).

primitives.name: Policy
primitives.definition: Concrete machine-readable rules applied in evaluation (material+hash).

primitives.name: Decision
primitives.definition: Formal evaluation result for an Event under Policy+Authority.

primitives.name: ReasonCode
primitives.definition: Stable explanation code for outcomes; semantics must not drift.

primitives.name: Effect
primitives.definition: Cross-boundary action/state change (attempted/applied) measurable.

primitives.name: Evidence
primitives.definition: Curated artifact set proving claims without rerun; hashed and indexed.

primitives.name: Run
primitives.definition: Single execution emitting records and artifacts; addressable unit.

primitives.name: Wave
primitives.definition: Orchestrated set of Runs under scenario; produces publish set.

primitives.name: Bundle
primitives.definition: Portable package of policy+runs+evidence with sealed manifest.

primitives.name: Verification
primitives.definition: Deterministic offline verification (schema+hash+invariants) PASS/FAIL.

primitives.name: Claim Registry
primitives.definition: Claim registry resolution for governance assertions and review.

primitives.name: Assertion Exec
primitives.definition: Evaluate governance assertions with deterministic reason codes.

primitives.name: Control Set
primitives.definition: Resolve and apply governance control sets for runtime decisions.

primitives.name: Invariant
primitives.definition: Always-true rule checked over timelines/records; produces findings.

primitives.name: Exception Handling
primitives.definition: Governed exception request and reconciliation lifecycle.

primitives.name: Scope
primitives.definition: Applicability perimeter (resources/actions) for policy/authority/claims.

primitives.name: Report Emit
primitives.definition: Deterministic report emission and artifact linking.

primitives.name: Disclosure
primitives.definition: Auditable declaration of redactions/omissions and motivations.
